recent.statement: NG=F will move up by at least 2% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: No captured signal shows NG=F moved up by at least 2% within the window, and such a move would have been captured.
No captured signal shows BZ=F closing 2.5% higher within the window, and such a move would have been recorded.

recent.statement: The Brent Crude price will drop by more than 5% in the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: Brent fell from 94.03 to 92.73 (-1.42%), far short of the required 5% drop.

recent.statement: NG=F will fall by at least 2% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: No captured signal concerns this forecast, so a 2% drop in NG=F cannot be confirmed within the window.

recent.statement: Oil prices will rise by 2% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: Brent Crude is down 8.26% rather than up 2%, showing oil prices fell during the window.

recent.statement: The price of WTI Crude Oil will rise to $90/bbl within the next 24h
recent.evidence: WTI Crude Oil is at 89.59 $/bbl, below the $90 threshold, so the forecast target was not reached.

recent.statement: The US crude oil price will rise by at least 2% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: S12 shows WTI Crude Oil (US crude) at 89.59 $/bbl, up +3.18%, exceeding the 2% threshold.

recent.statement: The price of CL=F will increase by at least 2% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: No signal shows CL=F rising 2%; the [now] crude anchor (Brent) instead fell 8.42%, indicating no such increase occurred.

recent.statement: WTI Crude Oil price will drop by at least 2% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: The latest [now] WTI signal shows the price at 88 $/bbl up +1.35%, not dropping 2%, and all prior signals show gains.

recent.statement: CL=F will increase by 2% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: No captured signal shows CL=F increased by 2% within the window, and a move that large would have been recorded.

recent.statement: A cyberattack will occur on a major oil company within the next week.
recent.evidence: No signal reports any cyberattack on an oil company; signals only show oil prices, FOMC schedules, and volatility indices.

recent.statement: A tropical cyclone will make landfall in the Philippines within the next week.
recent.evidence: The three named tropical cyclones (BERTHA, FAUSTO, ELIDA) are located far from the Philippines with zero population affected, and no signal shows any cyclone landfall in the Philippines.

self_calibration.note: Published headline probability is RAW conviction (unchanged); this is the honest companion. Calibration fixes overconfidence, not discrimination — see /calibration.
